parsed_review.summary: CP00-907 (risk A, 10 units, RP30.P02.M01.S06-RP30.P02.M02.S02) is a clean descriptor-only continuation of the embedded HRX People/HR evidence track, binding the LeaveWorkflow tail (8 rows) into the AttendanceWorkflow foundation (2 rows). Verified entirely through read-only inspection: (1) the pack is correctly positioned as the active live pack (closeout-pack-plan.json live_next_unit_id RP30.P02.M01.S06 / generated_start_pack_id CP00-907; binding chain CP906->CP907->CP908 intact); (2) the implementation (registry.js createHrxCp907LeaveAttendanceWorkflowBridgeDescriptor) is descriptor-only with every runtime/write/real-data flag false, HRX_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION spread in, and no fs-write/fetch/child_process usage anywhere in packages/hrx/src; (3) validators.js enforces coverage distribution, per-row leave/attendance runtime+write+real-data boundary drift, baseline decisions, Hermes emits_runtime_receipt=false, and Claude read_only=true/not-final-approver; (4) model.test.js asserts the 8/2 distribution, closed leave+attendance runtime, and that the committed contract JSON projection matches with zero validation errors; (5) authority boundary is correct -- Claude remains read-only independent reviewer (not final approver), Hermes H30 is a deterministic control-plane review gate not product runtime, and the pack explicitly defers runtime/enterprise-trust claims; (6) CP178+ hardening is satisfied (auth preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON, no forbidden tools, schema present, no invalid prior attempts). The pending/false states in the manifest, command-evidence, adjudication, and construction-inspection are the expected pre-review template state that this review gates. The full validation ladder is documented to be re-run before commit; substantive correctness is confirmed by inspection of source, tests, validators, and the generated contract. No production_ready overclaim, no real data, no writes, no runtime opening. Pack supports closeout. blocks_goal_closeout=false because this CP makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim and Claude's non-final-approval boundary is the expected, out-of-receipt authority boundary.
